title: KINGXBAR Smart Watch for Women, Waterproof IP68 1.3" Full Touch Screen Heart Rate Smart Watch with Body Temperature Measurement Sleep and Swim Tracker, Best Fitness Gift for Mother Lover Friends

Show you detailed data (Highest, Lowest, Average data) in YFit app. KINGXBAR smart watch guides your breathing and controls your anger and nervous, feel more relaxed by guiding you to do breathing exercises.

HD Full Touch Color Screen & Ultra-Long Battery Life - This activity tracker watch has a 1.4 inches TFT full-touch delicate color LCD display with 5 different levels of brightness that enable you the best visual experience, brighter and clearer, easier to read in shadow or sun. No bulky but bigger and brighter HD screen than ever. Use low power chipset and solve smart watch with short battery life issue, 15 days long battery life standby, 7 days of normal use.

With IP68 Waterproof Capacity - It is suitable for daily use without worrying about water damage, splash/rain resistant, not suitable for showering, bathing, swimming, snorkeling, water-related work and fishing, diving. DON'T WEAR IT IN HOT WATER OR TAKE BATH.

Smart Watch for Android Phones and iPhone - This smart watch can work with most of the smart phones in the market, only requiring that your phone’s system is above Android 5.0 or iOS 9.0. Notifications for incoming calls, texts, calendars and apps can be shown and read on this smart watch. You will never miss the messages that matter. title: Apple Watch Series 6 (GPS + Cellular, 40mm) - Graphite Stainless Steel Case with Black Sport Band (Renewed)
description: Monitor even more of your health with the Apple Watch Series 6. The Series 6 adds pulse oximetry to measure your blood's oxygen levels (SpO2) in just 15 seconds, as well as showing you your VO2 Max (maximum oxygen consumption) score. These indicators can provide early warnings to possible respiratory conditions. They are joined by an optical heart rate monitor and the ability to record your own ECG (electrocardiogram). The Series 6 can even detect and help you with healthy habits, such as hand-washing detection, which displays a 20-second animated countdown as you scrub. Fitness is a large part of staying healthy, and the Apple Watch has a variety of ways to assist you in reaching your goals. Activity Rings displays three rings that correspond to movement, exercise, and standing. Stay mobile throughout the day to hit new persol bests as you close the rings. You can also choose from a variety of workouts, such as running, swimming, yoga, cycling, and more; with the watch recording and tracking their specific metrics.Fitness and health metrics can be viewed, customized, and tracked on the Apple Watch's curved watch face that houses an always-on LTPO OLED Reti display. Even in direct sunlight you can view the screen with just a glance. As you move, choose from a variety of music, podcasts, and audiobooks to entertain and motivate you. For added fun, share your activity rings with friends and challenge them to 7-day activity competitions. Throughout the day, the Apple Watch Series 6 keeps you connected with the world around you. When paired with your iPhone, you can make calls and send texts directly from your wrist, vigate your surroundings with Maps, buy items with Apple Pay, and use your voice to interact with Siri. Made to last in almost any kind of weather, the Apple Watch Series 6 is water-resistant up to 164'. Use its 32GB of built-in storage for apps, watch faces, music, and more.
features: GPS + Cellular model lets you call, text, and get directions without your phone
Measure your blood oxygen with an all-new sensor and app
Check your heart rhythm with the ECG app
The Always-On Retina display is 2.5x brighter outdoors when your wrist is down
S6 SiP is up to 20% faster than Series 5
5GHz Wi-Fi and U1 Ultra Wideband chip
Track your daily activity on Apple Watch and see your trends in the Fitness app on iPhone
Measure workouts like running, walking, cycling, yoga, swimming, and dance
Swimproof design
Sync your favorite music, podcasts, and audiobooks

title: Kimnix Smart Watch 2022(Make Call Receive/Dial), 1.72 in HD Full Touch Screen Fitness Tracker with Bluetooth Call/Text/Heart Rate/SpO2/Sleep Monitor Smartwatch for Android iOS Mens Women (Black)

reviews.title: Fair features for the price
reviews.text:
First of all, you must keep in consideration that this is a $50 watch. That being said I will state what I consider are the pros and cons:

Pros
Price, battery life (more than the typical 1-day of apple watches), capable of answering and dialing within the watch (Bluetooth required) and blood oxygen and heart rate sensors.

Cons
Strap does not hold to watch and you must keep correcting it (solved with another strap?), blood pressure monitor? How???, you only have five watch faces at any given time but you can only replace one of the five - the others are permanently set, you get to edit the fourth one by adding a photo

Overall, there are cheaper and better watches like the WYZE (which I own and has better quality) but lack the phone answering and dialing features.

I will be replacing the strap with another type to see if it holds.

reviews.title: It does not keep all of the apps current. That is what I bought it for.
reviews.text: I bought this watch per my doctors request to keep health issues.  I only use it for this feature.  But to my amazement, It does not do as it states.  As I use only the medical aspects only I would expect it to work perfectly.

title: Garmin Forerunner 245, GPS Running Smartwatch with Advanced Dynamics, Berry
description: You do the running. Forerunner 245 does the thinking. This GPS smartwatch doesn’t just tell you where you ran. It tracks your stats, crunches the numbers and gets to know all about your performance, your running form, your training history and even your goals. All you need to focus on is putting one foot in front of the other.Bezel Material: fiber-reinforced polymer.
features: GPS running smartwatch with advanced training features
Battery life: up to 7 days in smartwatch mode and up to 24 hours in GPS mode
Get free adaptive training plans from Garmin Coach, or create your own custom workouts on our Garmin Connect online fitness community

Provides advanced running dynamics, including ground contact time balance, stride length, vertical ratio and more (When used with Running Dynamics Pod or HRM-Run or HRM-Tri monitors (sold separately))
Safety and tracking features include incident detection (during select activities) which sends your real-time location to emergency contacts through your paired compatible smartphone
Customize with free watch faces, apps and more from our Connect IQ store
Evaluates your current training status to indicate if you’re undertraining or overdoing it; offers additional performance monitoring features

reviews.title: great looking watch and great for running and biking
reviews.text:
I really like this watch. I replaced and old forerunner. (many generation old) It feels great and looks good since you can download and tailor the watch faces. I have downloaded music to it using MediaMonkey and it worked fine. So far, synching with earbuds Jaybird Tarah Pro and Vistas, has been good. The availability of watch faces and activity widgets is nice.
But
Be warned, it takes a little bit to set it up and understand how to understand how to download and update activity widgets. Updating of watch faces isn't too bad. Be patient and read the user guide, there is a lot of functionality you should be aware of whether you use them or not.

reviews.title: Great purchase
reviews.text:
Great, does all of the stuff running nerds love. Spotify is a hassle to sync when i want to adjust my playlists. This is especially annoying for podcast listeners such as myself. For this reason, I could have gone without the music upgrade and just used my phone but I still use the music feature a good amount for my shorter speed runs where I need the running jams!

But obviously I didn’t take a star because Garmin is the GOAT

reviews.title: Great watch
reviews.text: I bought this for my husband, an avid runner, for our anniversary. He is super into tracking, numbers, stats, etc. And he loves this watch! He previously had the Forerunner 235 Garmin but it was probably close to five years old. He had done alot of research on this one verses the newer models and this one still came out ahead and did not disappoint!

reviews.title: great fitness watch!
reviews.text: if you are looking for a smart watch, then this is not for you. however, if you want a fitness watch, then get this one! the gps tracking is great and accurate! it takes a little bit to get used to the buttons and where all the settings are, but i love that i can customize it to do just what i want in terms of display, audio outputs, etc. the band is comfortable and the watch face is a good size to see my stats while working out without being overly cumbersome (i'm a small 5'0" female). i love that it can play my spotify playlists! the only thing of note is that it will not be able to get any data such as weather, sms notifications, calendar updates, emails, phone calls, or send emergency assistance notices unless you have your phone with you and connected via bluetooth. the point of this watch for me was to keep my phone at home so those features don't work during my run, but it does do the fitness tracking and play previously setup spotify playlists, so it's exactly what i want and need. i don't use it for sleep tracking since i only wear it during workouts. love the heart rate monitoring too!

reviews.title: Sleep Tracking and Incoming Call Notification (on android) are EPIC failures
reviews.text:
Overwhelming 5 start reviews really make me skeptical that the reviews themselves are genuine....
Overall the watch is not bad but really needs functional work. The Android Dev/QA team should have some re-training/structuring after releasing this to the general public...
Sleep tracking shows me as sleeping if I am in bed relaxed watching tv during sleep hours, making it useless. I only receive an incoming call notification on the watch if my phone screen is unlocked...
missed calls come in every time..

no problems with GPS, Battery Life, Music , Bluetooth, feeds to 3rd party athletic tracking apps. all those work fine.

Not sure I would buy this again.

reviews.title: Great looking watch, battery life isn't what I expected
reviews.text: The major feature that seems missing from this watch is sleep tracking.  Reviews of the watch point to the limited battery life of the watch, and not being enough to track though an average night's sleep.

reviews.title: Measure your wrist before you buy.
reviews.text:
The blue silicone band was too small to go around my wrist. I called Skagen and they offered to sell me a bigger band. I told them I shouldn't have to pay extra for the watch to go on my wrist. They said since I bought it from Amazon there was nothing they could do. So sadly, this is getting returned.
I've worn plenty of watches before, and I don't have a fatty wrist. My wrist is 8 inches in circumference, which is average for a male.
The interesting thing about the size as I inspected and measured the watch, is that if you measure the watch laid out flat, 8 inches goes to the second to last notch, and it should fit fine. BUT -- the watch body itself obviously does not bend, which means in actual wear when the watch goes AROUND your wrist, you need an additional 3/4 to 1 inch for it to work.
It's basically like that one gallon jug I bought at Walmart, I got it home to make a drink mix in it just to realize that yes, it held one gallon, if you filled it to the absolute brim, at which point putting in the drink mix made it spill.
If your wrist is greater than 7 inches in circumference, I would not buy this watch.

reviews.title: Great replacement bands for the Fitbit Charge 5
reviews.text: These bands fit well on the Fitbit Charge 5, as a replacement for a damaged band or just to change up the look now and then.  They are easy to install and remove and they do give an entirely different look to the Charge 5. I have the Large size and they are the same length as the original band that came with the Fitbit.  The colors as presented in listing are accurate and, in fact, I liked the teal and purple even more when I saw them in person.  There's only one noticeable difference between these bands and the original band.  The original band is quite smooth while these bands have some friction to them when you run you fingers over them.  Neither texture is better than the other. They're just different. My original band is black and absent the difference in feel, there's no real distinction vs. the black band that I received in this six pack.

reviews.title: So glad I found this one!
reviews.text: I am so happy with the way this looks. It doesn’t look a traditional smart watch. It is more stylish. Would purchase again

reviews.title: Looks great but a pain to navigate
reviews.text: I ordered this watch because I love the style. I don’t care for the styles of most smart watches I’ve seen.  This watch looks great, very stylish!!  It comes with a tool to allow you to adjust the band length which is very helpful.  It keeps time perfectly.  It counts your steps and monitors sleep quality. The heart rate and BP monitors seem to be accurate as they are right on with my normal numbers. It measures sleep quality.  The hours I sleep is accurate but I don't know how to tell if the deep vs. light sleep is correct.  I assume its based on heartrate which seems to be accurate.  I cannot get the female app to sync, however,  I don’t use this feature. The apps are a pain to navigate through but aside from the female app they work well. The best part of this watch aside from its ascetics is that the charge lasts four to five days.

reviews.title: I wanted to love it
reviews.text: It really isn't the watches fault that I returned it.  It did all it said it would do, the Alexa feature worked, the heart rate monitor worked , the picture was bright, among other things. I didn't like that if you use the timer ( which I use frequently at my job) you can't go back to the main screen, if you do it turns off the timer, but you can use Alexa as a timer and don't have to stay on the same screen so there is a work around. I was also dissapointed with the fact there were only 4 face choices.  But, the reason I returned it is that my phone is older and it wears down my battery quickly and was causing cpu (?) overload warnings.

reviews.title: Solid watch
reviews.text:
Watch came well packaged and was easy to set up and pair using the provided instructions on the item page. It was made well and kept good time. I have a Motorola phone and it would not stay paired with my phone. The sleep feature never seemed to work because it was constantly disconnecting from the app. It may be a good choice for someone else, but I returned it.

The battery life was excellent and it was a well constructed and comfortable watch

title: Samsung Galaxy Watch3 2020 Smartwatch (Bluetooth + Wi-Fi + GPS) International Model (Bronze, 41mm)
description: The only smartwatch with a precise rotating bezel for navigating between apps. Larger screen, but lighter and thinner for more comfort: With its subtle rotating bezel, it's easier to see and focus on what's important on an even larger screen. The Galaxy Watch3 is thinner and smaller, and up to 15% lighter with a sophisticated look that, thanks to its reduced frame, is perfect for the wrist. Breathe easier with built-in oxygen measurement: The Galaxy Watch3 uses red LEDs and infrared rays to calculate your SpO2 levels or the amount of oxygen in your blood. Because oxygen saturation is directly related to physical activity, you can use the Galaxy Watch3 to push your routine to the next level. Enjoy a better sleep: The Galaxy Watch3 records your active sleep, deep sleep, and total sleep time to keep track and improve the quality of your sleep. Samsung partnered with the National Sleep Foundation to include educational content and provide tips on your device to help you sleep better. Do your best and then repeat it: With over 120 home workout programs to choose from, the Galaxy Watch3 helps you move your body at your own pace. Pick one from your phone and stream it on your TV. It will show you your heart rate in real-time while you exercise. Use the Ridge Sport strap for even more accurate stats. Continue your conversations: When you receive a new message, the Galaxy Watch3 shows your conversation history so you can follow them. You can also view images without needing an extra touch, making it easier to keep up while you're working out. Add some context with the smart answer: Respond to messages at all times and without missing a beat. The Galaxy Watch3's smart reply analyzes the texts and photos you receive and gives you possible answers in different categories, such as food, celebrations, selfies and much more.

reviews.title: does what it's supposed to
reviews.text:
*edit: I've found a con and it bugged me enough to update my review. If your phone is on "Do not disturb" and you leave your phone on at night for emergency calls, the watch still vibrates with every text, regardless of whether they're on your emergency list. This is annoying when you're a light sleeper and you get a text for go cart racing or Dairy Queen at 4 am. I'm assuming it would still go off with a call as well.

I have a Garmin Vivofit that for some reason the notification vibrations will work for a couple months, then stop working for a while, then randomly start working again. I was sick of this, and went looking for something not too expensive and found this. I'm still getting used to it, but it vibrates when I get a call or text, so I'm happy so far. The band is very flexible and comfortable, which is nice, since my Vivofit band was a little stiff.

reviews.title: Great watch for the price.
reviews.text: After buying the earlier brands of Fitbit which I loved. I bought myself a Luxe on sale for $100 last December 2021. It didn’t record anything accurately and just didn’t function the way it should. CS was if no help. I switched to Amazfit!  This is a great watch, the battery has lasted over a week. It accurately tells time, my heart rate, steps and I love this watch. It’s great and is so light on my wrist!!

reviews.title: Terrible smart watch, inaccurate and waste of time/money
reviews.text: After 5 months of use I’m done with this. It seems to be getting less accurate every workout. It can’t even get step counts correct. The accuracy of the heart beat monitoring varies too. The sleep monitor is often wrong about awake times so I don’t believe the other sleep data either. The only thing I can believe with this watch is the time of day.

reviews.title: Good budget watch if you don't need always-on display
reviews.text: Overall, this is a very reasonably priced watch with a lot of functionality. However, the fact that it doesn't have the always-on screen option was a deal breaker for me and I had to replace it with one of their other models.

reviews.title: Did not like this nearly as much as my original amazfit bip watch
reviews.text:
I bought an Amazfit Bip watch a year or more ago and have really been enjoying it The app tracks all my cycling trips and the sleep monitoring is useful. Iam really a big fan of the long battery life of the older model.

This watch has a 9 day battery life which is not nearly as long as the 30 days of the original watch.

This watch meausres blood oxygen levels but taking the measurement requires holding your wrist it the right position and takes much longer that the finger tip device Ihave.

I did not exhaustively evaluate the new app that came with this phone but it did not look nearly as good as the original app.

reviews.title: Awesome watch for the price!  On 2nd thought, maybe not so awesome
reviews.text:
I received this watch, opened it up and set it to charge for a couple of hours. I then followed the instructions to download the app onto my phone. I went to settings and pretty much turned the settings on for everything so that I could see if it would work. It does. I can check the weather, my temperature, blood pressure, oxygen, pulse and steps. I can set it to a sport activity and there are multiple ones. I was able to go to settings on the phone app and select 1 out of 152 different picture settings (and I can change it as much as I want to). It came with the 2 bands- pink and light brown or tan. I have only worn the pink band so far and it is soft. The charge on the battery lasted a week, which is nice because if you just remember to charge it for 2 hours once a week on the same day, that would be easy to remember. I have not really used the camera function but did somehow try it out as I twisted my wrist, and the phone camera took a picture. I have not used the music part of the watch either, but that is because I do not have a music option on my phone right now. The only issue that I had was connecting it to Google Fit so that I could attach that to the Walker Tracker App (which I use for competitions for # of steps). That is okay though because I can manually put the daily steps in. Overall, I would definitely recommend this watch. I have never had an Apple or Samsung Watch, but with this one, if it breaks, I won't be crying like I would with one of the expensive watches... and it works for what I want it for!

6/3/22- Update- this watch has been acting nuts the last 2 weeks. I was hoping it would work itself out, but it has not. The watch randomly buzzes and/or blinks. Sometimes it zeroes itself out. I am getting really frustrated. I have asked a question above to see if I can get help, so I will see what happens and let you know. I have only had it for maybe 6 weeks.

reviews.title: Doesn’t work correct
reviews.text: It doesn’t read while I’m asleep disconnects and doesn’t detect. And apparently I have hypothermia cause it constantly reads my temp at 95.7. Very sad and disappointed . Good battery life tho

reviews.title: Soo good!
reviews.text: I was skeptical because of the price but wow! I've had the expensive $200 watch before and this one works better. I can customize the face, the battery lasts days, the band does not bother me when I sleep like the other bulky watches, very easy set up. Amazing buy and I will be buying my children both one to monitor their sleep. Get it!!

reviews.title: Super great! As good or better than Apple Watch.
reviews.text: Great!

reviews.title: Warning: Do not trust medical readings!
reviews.text:
When I first opened this and changed the pink silicone strap to the rose gold steel strap, I thought it was pretty, albeit quite large for my tiny wrist. But I got this specifically for the health monitoring and wanted something that looked a little classy. Well, the only thing that reads correctly on my watch is the time.

I downloaded the app and it wants you to create an account and to allow access to everything personal on your phone. No thanks. I was not interested in receiving texts, calls, and alerts anyway. I just wanted to monitor my pulse, blood pressure, and steps. Everything else was supposed to be a bonus for me.

I had two separate doctor appointments yesterday and was alarmed when I compared the findings.
* Steps: When we discussed my new "smart"watch, the one doctor told me he's a runner and tries to get 10,000 steps daily even when he works. Apparently I walked about a third of that despite driving most of the day. GO ME!
* Temp: In the prior two days I wore this, my temperature has always read in the 95-96 degree range. Temp at both offices was NORMAL.
* O2 stats are probably ok, but that wasn't checked, at least that I paid attention to.
* BP: Here's a HUGE problem. It has consistently been reading at 128/76 +/- only 4 points on systolic or diastolic. But in both offices, my systolic was wrong by more than 30-35 points! I just came off BP medicine and needed to monitor it, so this was shocking, to say the least.
* Pulse: The other consistently incorrect reading. The watch has been reading between 90-110 at rest. This is alarmingly conceivable considering my health concern, which is why I went for one appointment to have it checked. However, after an EKG, my heart is fine and my pulse was actually around 70-80 at both offices after a little walking.
*Sleep: Since I have several issues with sleep, I looked forward to this info. Apparently I sleep while at my computer drinking coffee in the morning, as well as on part of my long drive home yesterday.

So not that it even matters at this point, but the location and weather would never "set" on the app or watch either, even though I specifically turned on my location just for your app. It just stayed blank with lines. I guess that's better than telling me it's snowing when it's really 70 degrees, considering your other reliable results.

Honestly, if you're going to market this as an activity tracker with health monitoring, then it should be able to give at least SOMEWHAT reliable readings. Mine weren't even close. I checked my temp, pulse, and BP manually the moment after it was taken in both offices to verify accuracy and it was so far off I thought I may be in jeopardy. So a big thank you for causing additional anxiety thinking my BP was only slightly high when it was way too high, and giving me tachycardia when it was only a bit elevated. Add my freezing temp and I should probably be close to death.

This is one of the most disturbing, nerve-wracking items I've ever gotten on Amazon. Or probably anywhere.

title: SAMSUNG GEAR S3 FRONTIER Smartwatch 46MM (Bluetooth Only) - Dark Grey (Renewed)
description:
Stay in touch, without reaching for your phone, with the smartwatch that’s engineered for adventure. Convenience being the key feature, the watch offers fitness tracking, app access and watch face customization. With the Gear S3, you can pay right from your wrist and track your location as well. The Gear S3 is compatible with both Android and iOS smartphones.
Never Miss a Moment
The Gear S3 is Bluetooth connected so you can receive texts, emails, and alerts without reaching for your phone. With a built-in speaker and microphone, you can also make and answer calls hands-free. Just rotate the distinctive steel bezel to easily access apps and notifications so you can instantly check texts, updates, and more.
Pay with Your Watch
With Samsung Pay on your Gear S3, you can easily make payments with your watch almost anywhere you can swipe or tap a credit card - even without your phone or wallet.
Get Out There
Whether you’re off-roading or exploring a new city, the built-in GPS tracks your activity and makes it easy to share your location.
Designed for The Elements
The military-grade Gear S3 is tough enough to handle the elements. It resists water, dust, extreme temperatures, and the occasional drop so you can venture out with confidence.
Go for Days
Go nonstop for days on a single charge.4 When it’s time to recharge, the wireless charger lets you power up without slowing down.
Tailored to You
Make the Gear S3 your own. Customize your watch face, choosing from thousands of striking colors and designs. Plus easily swap out the standard 22mm band.
features: The Samsung Gear S3 has an always-on watch face and distinctive steel bezel that you can rotate to access apps and notifications.
The Samsung Gear S3 has an always-on watch face and distinctive steel bezel that you can rotate to access apps and notifications.
Text, call and get notifications directly from your watch through your Bluetooth connected smartwatch.
Access the apps you use every day on your Gear S3. Catch a ride, check scores, stream music, and more.
With the built-in S Health app, track your steps, monitor your heart rate, and more.

reviews.title: Just over 90 days and it completely died
reviews.text: Rrally liked this watch at first. The battery did not last very long, even after adjusting settings. Thought it was the battery so we bought a new one, nope just completely died now. The new battery is fine, charges up and works, but the watch will not even turn on with new battery in it.

reviews.title: Love this
reviews.text: Love this watch

reviews.title: Husband LOVES This
reviews.text: I bought this for my husband for his birthday and Father's Day and he loves it. He used to have a Galaxy S9, but dropped it into a lake while we were fishing *face palm*. But he ended up getting an old S7 from a family member and the watch connects with that as well. The only complaint I have heard from him is about the band, which was easy and cheap to replace with a metal material that didn't stick to his wrist as much as the rubbery band that comes with the watch.

reviews.title: Great looking watch!
reviews.text: I bought this for my husband and he loves it!  He answers his phone and text messages right on the watch.  He doesn't have an excuse not to answer me now :D

reviews.title: Good watch
reviews.text:
I bought this when it first came out, I needed it's walking and stair climbing metrics.

It's big, heavy, and if you're at all active, it's on the charge cradle for around 1 hour. The big, heavy part, some like it, I wish it were a little smaller. The battery life, it's an early version, I didn't expect much, my next watch, 20 day minimum.

All in all, it's a very functional watch, feature laden, and good, as long as Samsung supports the OS
